essay_questions.title: Male Reproductive Anatomy and Function
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the anatomy of the male reproductive system, including the testes, epididymis, vas deferens, accessory glands, and penis. Explain the function of each structure in sperm production, maturation, storage, and delivery. Include the roles of Sertoli cells and Leydig cells in testicular function.

essay_questions.title: Female Reproductive Anatomy and Function
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the anatomy of the female reproductive system, including the ovaries, fallopian tubes, uterus, cervix, and vagina. Explain the function of each structure in gamete production, transport, fertilization, and fetal development. Include the structure and function of the endometrium and its cyclic changes.

essay_questions.title: Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Gonadal Axis
essay_questions.prompt: Explain the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal (HPG) axis in detail. Describe the hormones involved (GnRH, LH, FSH, testosterone, estrogen, progesterone, inhibin), their sites of production, target tissues, and feedback mechanisms. Compare the HPG axis in males and females, highlighting the differences in regulation.

essay_questions.title: Spermatogenesis
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the process of spermatogenesis in detail, including the stages from spermatogonia to mature spermatozoa. Explain the hormonal regulation of spermatogenesis and the role of Sertoli cells. Discuss the duration of spermatogenesis and factors that can affect sperm production.

essay_questions.title: Oogenesis and Follicular Development
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the process of oogenesis from fetal development through adulthood. Explain follicular development, including the different types of follicles and their characteristics. Discuss the two-cell, two-gonadotropin theory of estrogen synthesis and the role of the zona pellucida.

essay_questions.title: The Menstrual Cycle
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the menstrual cycle in detail, including the ovarian cycle (follicular phase, ovulation, luteal phase) and the endometrial cycle (menstrual, proliferative, and secretory phases). Explain the hormonal changes throughout the cycle and how they coordinate the events of the cycle. Include the mechanism of the LH surge.

essay_questions.title: Fertilization
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the process of fertilization from sperm deposition to fusion of pronuclei. Explain capacitation, the acrosome reaction, sperm-zona pellucida binding, the blocks to polyspermy (fast and slow), and completion of meiosis in the oocyte. Discuss the timing and location of fertilization.

essay_questions.title: Early Embryonic Development
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the events of early embryonic development from the zygote to the blastocyst stage. Explain cleavage, compaction, blastocyst formation, and differentiation of the inner cell mass and trophoblast. Discuss the process of hatching from the zona pellucida.

essay_questions.title: Implantation and Placentation
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the process of implantation, including the stages and the interaction between the blastocyst and endometrium. Explain the development of the placenta, including chorionic villi formation and the structure and function of the placental barrier. Discuss the hormones produced by the placenta and their functions.

essay_questions.title: Pregnancy Physiology
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the physiological changes that occur during pregnancy, including hormonal changes (hCG, progesterone, estrogen, hPL), cardiovascular changes, hematological changes, respiratory changes, and gastrointestinal changes. Explain the purpose of these adaptations for supporting the pregnancy.

essay_questions.title: Parturition
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the process of parturition (labor and delivery), including the three stages of labor. Explain the hormonal and mechanical factors that initiate and maintain labor, including the roles of estrogen, progesterone, oxytocin, prostaglandins, and the Ferguson reflex. Discuss the mechanisms that prevent excessive bleeding after delivery.

essay_questions.title: Lactation
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the anatomy of the breast and the process of lactation. Explain breast development during pregnancy, the hormones involved in milk production (prolactin) and milk ejection (oxytocin), and the composition of colostrum and mature milk. Discuss the mechanisms that regulate milk production and the benefits of breastfeeding.

essay_questions.title: Reproductive Endocrinology
essay_questions.prompt: Explain the synthesis and regulation of the major reproductive hormones (testosterone, estrogen, progesterone). Describe the steroidogenic pathways and the enzymes involved. Discuss the mechanisms of hormone transport in blood, including protein binding, and the mechanisms of hormone action at target cells.

essay_questions.title: Male Infertility
essay_questions.prompt: Discuss the causes, evaluation, and treatment of male infertility. Describe the components of semen analysis and what abnormalities may be found. Explain the various factors that can impair spermatogenesis and sperm function, including genetic, hormonal, anatomical, environmental, and lifestyle factors.

essay_questions.title: Female Infertility
essay_questions.prompt: Discuss the causes, evaluation, and treatment of female infertility. Describe the various factors that can impair female fertility, including ovulatory disorders, tubal factors, endometriosis, uterine abnormalities, and age-related decline in ovarian reserve. Explain the diagnostic tests used in evaluating female infertility.

essay_questions.title: Contraception
essay_questions.prompt: Discuss the various methods of contraception available, including hormonal methods (combined and progestin-only), barrier methods, intrauterine devices, emergency contraception, and permanent methods. Explain the mechanisms of action, effectiveness, advantages, and disadvantages of each method. Include both male and female contraceptive options.

essay_questions.title: Menopause
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the physiological changes that occur during menopause and the perimenopausal transition. Explain the hormonal changes (declining estrogen, rising FSH and LH) and their effects on various body systems. Discuss the symptoms of menopause, long-term health consequences, and treatment options including hormone replacement therapy.

essay_questions.title: Assisted Reproductive Technologies
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the various assisted reproductive technologies (ART) available, including in vitro fertilization (IVF), intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI), intrauterine insemination (IUI), and third-party reproduction (donor gametes, surrogacy). Explain the indications for each procedure, the steps involved, success rates, and ethical considerations.

essay_questions.title: Sexual Differentiation
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the process of sexual differentiation in humans, including genetic sex determination, gonadal differentiation, and development of internal and external genitalia. Explain the roles of SRY, testosterone, AMH, and DHT in male development, and what happens in the absence of these factors. Discuss common disorders of sexual development.

essay_questions.title: Puberty
essay_questions.prompt: Describe the physiological changes that occur during puberty in both males and females. Explain the hormonal mechanisms that initiate puberty, including the roles of kisspeptin, GnRH, and gonadotropins. Discuss the physical changes (growth spurt, development of secondary sexual characteristics, gonadal maturation) and the variations in timing of puberty.
